fact: The first official Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground.
India won its first ICC Cricket World Cup in 1983 under the captaincy of Kapil Dev.
The ICC Men's Cricket World Cup is held every four years.
Sachin Tendulkar is known as the 'Master Blaster' and scored 100 international centuries.
The Ashes is a historic Test cricket series played between Australia and England.
A cricket team has 11 players on the field.
The ICC was founded in 1909 and governs international cricket.
The first ICC Men's T20 World Cup was held in South Africa in 2007.
India won the inaugural ICC Men's T20 World Cup in 2007.
Virat Kohli has captained India in all three international formats.
A century in cricket means a batter scores 100 or more runs in one innings.
The Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG) is one of the world's largest cricket stadiums.
The first FIFA World Cup was held in Uruguay in 1930.
Uruguay won the inaugural FIFA World Cup.
The FIFA World Cup is held every four years.
A football team has 11 players on the field.
Lionel Messi captained Argentina to the 2022 FIFA World Cup title.
Cristiano Ronaldo has won multiple UEFA Champions League titles.
The UEFA Champions League is Europe's premier club football competition.
The Ballon d'Or is awarded annually to one of the world's best football players.
A standard football match consists of two halves of 45 minutes each.
The offside rule prevents attackers from gaining an unfair advantage near the opponent's goal.
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup more times than any other nation.
The penalty spot is located 12 yards from the goal line.
Basketball was invented by Dr. James Naismith in 1891.
A basketball team has five players on the court.
An NBA game consists of four quarters.
The NBA was founded in 1946.
Michael Jordan won six NBA championships with the Chicago Bulls.
A successful shot from beyond the three-point line is worth three points.
Free throws are worth one point each.
The basketball hoop is 10 feet above the floor.
The Olympic basketball tournament includes men's and women's competitions.
The point guard is often responsible for directing the team's offense.
A slam dunk is a shot where the player scores by putting the ball directly through the hoop.
The FIBA Basketball World Cup is organized by the International Basketball Federation.
There are four Grand Slam tournaments each year.
Wimbledon is the oldest tennis tournament.
The Australian Open is played on hard courts.
The French Open is played on clay courts.
Wimbledon is played on grass courts.
The US Open is played on hard courts.
A tennis match can be played in best-of-three or best-of-five sets depending on the event.
A player must win a set by at least two games unless a tiebreak decides the set.
Roger Federer won 20 Grand Slam singles titles.
Rafael Nadal is especially known for his success at the French Open.
Novak Djokovic has won multiple Grand Slam singles titles.
The ATP governs the main men's professional tennis tour.
The Thomas Cup is the world men's team badminton championship.
India won its first Thomas Cup title in 2022.
The BWF stands for Badminton World Federation.
Badminton is played with a shuttlecock.
A badminton singles court is narrower than a doubles court.
A badminton match is usually played as the best of three games.
Each game is normally played to 21 points.
P. V. Sindhu won a silver medal at the 2016 Olympic Games.
Saina Nehwal won an Olympic bronze medal in 2012.
The All England Open is one of the oldest badminton tournaments.
The Uber Cup is the world women's team badminton championship.
The badminton net is 1.55 meters high at the posts.
